era_context: The Old Kingdom of Egypt (Dynasty VI) is in its final century. The Akkadian Empire under Naram-Sin is at its height. The Indus Valley Civilization is in its mature, prosperous phase. In China, the Xia dynasty (if historical) would be in its early period, though archaeological evidence remains contested.

events.title: Pepi II of Egypt — continued reign and provincial autonomy
events.description: Pepi II's reign continues. The decentralization of power to provincial governors (nomarchs) is well-established, with local elites accumulating wealth and authority. The pharaonic state remains functional but increasingly dependent on provincial cooperation. Trade expeditions to Nubia and the Levant continue, as documented in official inscriptions.
events.certainty_note: The general context is well-attested; specific events in this year are not individually documented.
cross_references: Part of the Old Kingdom decline, -2510 to -2480

events.title: Naram-Sin of Akkad — imperial campaigns and consolidation
events.description: Naram-Sin continues military campaigns to consolidate Akkadian control over Mesopotamia, the Levant, and parts of Anatolia. Inscriptions record victories and the subjugation of rebellious regions. The Akkadian administrative system spreads throughout the empire, with Akkadian language used in official contexts.
events.certainty_note: The general pattern of Naram-Sin's reign is well-documented; specific events in this year are not individually attested.
cross_references: Part of Akkadian imperial expansion, -2254 to -2218 BCE

events.title: Indus Valley Civilization — mature phase prosperity
events.description: The Indus Valley Civilization continues in its mature phase with thriving urban centers, extensive trade networks, and sophisticated craft production. Standardized weights, measures, and seals indicate sophisticated systems of commerce and administration.
events.certainty_note: The mature Indus phase is archaeologically well-established; specific events in this year are not documented.
cross_references: Continuation of mature Indus phase, c. -2600 to -1900

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. Chronological uncertainty remains significant due to competing chronologies.
historiographic_note: This year falls within a period of sparse documentation. Surviving sources are primarily monumental inscriptions and administrative texts, which are selective and propagandistic. Specific events in this year are largely unknown.

graph_edges.note: Pepi II's reign continues in parallel with the previous year
